cards.scope: Five controlled task families through one offline_deterministic lifecycle.

evidence_grounding.note: Verifier checks evidence fields, but the offline agent path still emits deterministic capability/evidence hints rather than target-grounded reads.

repair_attribution.note: Each scenario records A1 feedback type and a distinct repair action before A2.

regression_safety.note: Regression is measured by verifier score and strengthened by separate negative controls, not by a broad held-out clean-target suite.

transferability.note: The same offline suite spans upload, auth, config, API review, and one non-security data-quality task.

repeatability.note: The offline path is deterministic and artifact-backed; reruns should be stable modulo timestamps.

cost_budget.note: Per-scenario summaries include latency; cost_budget_score is synthetic rather than measured from real model/token usage.

verifier_robustness.note: Robustness is supported only indirectly here; explicit unsupported-evidence/false-positive checks live in negative_controls.json.

human_plausibility.note: No systematic human rating is stored for whether A2 reports are materially more useful than A1.

cards.claim_boundary: Strong controlled lifecycle evidence, not open-world semantic quality evidence.

cards.scope: Deterministic non-oracle local semantic backend over five controlled task families.

outcome_delta.note: A2 passes 5/5, but only upload shows a repair loop; four tasks already pass at A1.

evidence_grounding.note: The backend reads target text, emits trace.jsonl read events, and grounds findings with detector hits.

repair_attribution.note: Only upload currently demonstrates verifier-triggered capability repair on this backend.

regression_safety.note: No dedicated clean-target local-semantic regression suite is stored yet.

transferability.note: The interface spans five families, but feedback diversity is low: only pass/no_op and one missing_capability case.

repeatability.note: This path is deterministic heuristic code with stable target reads.

cost_budget.note: Latency is small and there is no external token cost.

verifier_robustness.note: Verifier remains deterministic; robustness against semantic-but-wrong heuristics is only lightly tested.

human_plausibility.note: No manual review card exists for whether the heuristic reports are meaningfully useful beyond contract compliance.

cards.claim_boundary: Backend-interface and target-grounding evidence, not proof of autonomous semantic reasoning.

cards.scope: Controlled checks for unsupported evidence and false-positive rejection.

outcome_delta.note: This slice is about verifier refusal behavior, not task improvement.

evidence_grounding.note: Unsupported evidence is explicitly rejected when evidence_span is absent from the target.

repair_attribution.note: The clean-config control demonstrates gate behavior after a false-positive style repair, but not a full A1/A2 loop.

regression_safety.note: Always-append findings are rejected on a clean config; empty typed output can pass when expected issue set is empty.

transferability.note: Only two negative controls exist so far.

repeatability.note: These controls are deterministic and should be exactly reproducible.

cost_budget.note: No external model cost is involved.

verifier_robustness.note: This is the strongest direct evidence that verifier/gate can reject unsupported evidence and false positives.

human_plausibility.note: No human audit artifacts accompany the negative controls.

cards.claim_boundary: Narrow robustness evidence for two adversarial patterns, not a broad adversarial verification benchmark.

cards.scope: Controlled strategy comparison across upload and config tasks.

outcome_delta.note: Typed repair plus gate reaches PASS on both scenarios; naive strategies fail or incur regressions on config.

evidence_grounding.note: Ablation reuses the same controlled verifier path; it does not independently validate semantic grounding.

repair_attribution.note: Different repair choices produce measurably different outcomes under the same inputs.

regression_safety.note: Always-append and naive-regenerate show regression_count > 0 on config; gate rejects those paths.

transferability.note: Only two scenarios are included in the executable ablation.

cost_budget.note: Synthetic cost_budget_score differentiates strategies, but it is not calibrated to true runtime or token spend.

verifier_robustness.note: The verifier distinguishes regressions here, but broader adversarial checks remain outside this table.

human_plausibility.note: No human comparison of strategy outputs is recorded.

cards.claim_boundary: Useful controlled attribution evidence, not a benchmark-scale ablation.

cards.scope: Three-run repeatability smoke for the Harbor upload LLM repair loop.

outcome_delta.note: All three runs show A1 fail and A2 pass with reward delta +1.0.

evidence_grounding.note: Repeatability summarizes verifier outcomes and model usage; grounding remains delegated to the underlying run artifacts.

repair_attribution.note: Failure reason and missing-capability set are stable across repeats.

regression_safety.note: This slice checks stability of the same loop, not regression on unrelated clean tasks.

transferability.note: Repeatability is measured only on upload.

repeatability.note: A1 all fail, A2 all pass, reward stable, failure reason stable across three runs.

cost_budget.note: average total tokens per loop=2191.33, average combined latency ms per loop=24702.33

verifier_robustness.note: Stable failure types reduce the chance of arbitrary verifier drift, but do not exhaust robustness concerns.

human_plausibility.note: No repeated human evaluation accompanies the loop repeats.

cards.claim_boundary: Small repeatability smoke for one Harbor upload loop, not a broad prompt-sensitivity study.
